description: Cross-benchmark evaluation of all GUARD-Net checkpoints

kim2018.description: Kim et al. 2018 HT-PAMDA (HT2+HT3 held-out test)

easydesign_internal.description: EasyDesign (Huang et al. 2024) internal log-k split
easydesign_internal.note: Original EasyDesign test split discarded due to measurement mismatch (log-k train vs raw fluorescence test)

results.name: Kim-only (RLPA)
results.notes: Previous production checkpoint. Strong on cis-cleavage, near-zero on trans-cleavage.

results.name: Multi-dataset no DA
results.notes: NEW production checkpoint (guard_net_diagnostic.pt). Best trans-cleavage performance while retaining cis-cleavage.

results.name: Multi-dataset + DA
results.notes: Domain adversarial hurts: GRL forces invariance that destroys domain-specific signal.

results.name: Phase1 + RNA-FM
results.notes: RNA-FM without RLPA. No trans-cleavage transfer.

results.name: EasyDesign-only
results.notes: Trans-cleavage specialist. Outperformed by multi-dataset pooling on its own data.

key_findings: Cis-cleavage and trans-cleavage are nearly uncorrelated: Kim-only model scores rho=0.045 on trans-cleavage.
Multi-dataset pooling without DA gives the best all-rounder: rho=0.49 cis + rho=0.55 trans.
Domain adversarial training is counter-productive: destroys trans-cleavage signal (0.39 vs 0.55).
EasyDesign-only is outperformed by multi-dataset pooling on its own test set (0.51 vs 0.55).
EasyDesign dataset has a train/test measurement mismatch (log-k vs fluorescence) that we identified and corrected.
